scores.materiality_reasoning: Enslavement is the ultimate loss of bodily integrity and property; liberty is a primary material good.
scores.generality_reasoning: Protection from enslavement is a broad, fundamental legal protection.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Selling an individual into slavery has lifelong consequences for their status and freedom.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Resident alien (metic) status is a legal birth-status or long-term residency status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author (Diogenes Laertius) is writing centuries after the Classical Athenian events described.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the specific legal consequence (sale for tax) as a known historical event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an alien does not inherently mean one is liable to be sold for tax debt.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Sale of a whole family into slavery is a total loss of bodily and material autonomy.
scores.generality_reasoning: Personal liberty is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Slavery is a permanent/lifelong change in legal identity.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group 'Slaves' refers to a structural legal status (though the text implies they were freedmen).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author recounts a biographical event from centuries prior.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a factual account of a family's historical legal transition.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slavery does not inherently entail being sold due to a parent's tax fraud.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Inheriting 'all he had' involves tangible property and wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to inherit property is a foundational economic axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Inheritance provides a permanent shift in one's material standing and freedom.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The text recounts the biography of a 3rd-century BC philosopher.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The will and its result are reported as an attested biographical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a slave typically excludes inheritance; this rule describes an exception.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to indict for impiety is a legal power with direct material effects (exile/death).
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the legal procedure of religious indictment.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The power is attached to the long-term holding of a specific office.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Hierophant is a stable, often hereditary or lifelong religious priesthood.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical event involving Aristotle (4th century BC).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Records a specific historical prosecution as an event of record.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Indicting others is not the definition of a hierophant.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to marry has direct material effects on property and social standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Marriage is a foundational societal right/axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Marital status is a lifelong/long-term structural reality.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Dependent/concubine status is a stable legal/social condition.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Biographical anecdote from the 4th century BC.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a specific instance within a biographical narrative.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a dependent doesn't automatically mean one cannot marry without consent in every society.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from legal prosecution is a concrete legal exemption.
scores.generality_reasoning: Freedom from legal prosecution spans many facets of life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A single indictment is a bounded legal episode.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Philosopher is an occupation/identity acquired by training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical event concerning Aristotle.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to a specific attempted legal case.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Prosecution for impiety is not part of the definition of philosopher.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The choice of spouse impacts property, lineage, and social standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to marry is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Choice of spouse (or lack thereof) has a lifelong impact.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group defined by gender (women) and age (youth), both immutable at a given point.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Quoting Aristotle's will from the 4th century BC.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a primary legal document (Aristotle's will) as evidence.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman doesn't tautologically mean one lacks marital choice.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Involves silver (talent) and slaves (property) given as maintenance.
scores.generality_reasoning: Financial maintenance and dowry are broad economic rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Provides the basis for a life-long financial transition/new marriage.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by gender (women) and status (partner of the deceased).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Based on a 4th-century BC document.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Text provides the specific text of the testamentary gift.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Maintenance is not part of the definition of a concubine.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical freedom and cash grants are direct material resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to freedom is the most general/foundational right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Manumission is a lifelong change in legal status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slaves are a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Based on a 4th-century BC document.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct citation of a legal instrument (the will).
scores.tautology_reasoning: Freedom is the opposite of the group definition (slave).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from being sold preserves one's physical and social situation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific protection against being 'liquidated' as an asset.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds until manumission or reaching the 'proper age'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Structural legal status as household servants/slaves.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Based on a 4th-century BC document.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct citation of the owner's legal instructions.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Protection from sale is not inherent to being a slave.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to preside over a school is a professional/economic right; death penalty is physical.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to public office/professional practice in a field.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The law restricted the entire career duration of the group.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (philosopher) is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites a specific historic Athenian decree (Sophocles' law).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim reports the enactment of a specific statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lack of Senate permission is not part of the definition of a philosopher.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom of movement and residency in a city (Athens) are material rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right of residency is a foundational civil right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as the repeal stands and the individuals remain.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical account of the repeal of Sophocles' law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the repeal and voting as factual events.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Residency is not definitionally tied to philosophy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A public funeral is a ceremonial honor with minor material side effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific ritual / institution.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A one-time event occurring at the end of life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Prominent status among philosophers is a durable identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical event concerning Theophrastus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as an 'all Athenians' event, though biographical framing might be illustrative.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Public funerals are not the definition of a philosopher.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Dowries are tangible financial assets that enable marriage.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to marry in youth is a broad social/life outcome.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Remaining unmarried 'past the flower of age' is a permanent life shift.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender (women) is immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a Classical Greek social reality.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Framed as a rhetorical observation of a 'grievous burden'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Youthful marriage is not in the definition of 'woman'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Personal liberty (emancipation) is a primary material/legal resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Fundamental right to freedom.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Manumission is a permanent/lifelong change in status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical document (will of Theophrastus).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the specific text of the legal manumission.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Freedom is the opposite of the group definition (slave).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Slaves are tangible property; owning them is a material right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property ownership is a foundational economic axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Exclusion from property ownership is usually lifelong/structural for slaves.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical document (Theophrastus's will).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The will treats the slaves as property to be bequeathed, a legal fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: In this context, being a slave ('property') definitionally excludes the right to own self or property.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Land ownership/possession is a foundational material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to own land is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Restrictions on land tenure are structural and long-term.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic (alien) status is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical document from the Peripatetic school.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the conditions of a legal trust/will.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Restricted land tenure is not definitionally part of being an alien.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Liberty (freedom) is a primary material good.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational civil status.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Manumission is a lifelong change in legal personhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Citing a historical will.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the specific condition of the manumission.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Conditional freedom is not the definition of a slave.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Full citizenship status involves myriad material legal rights (voting, land, property protection).
scores.generality_reasoning: Citizenship is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Exclusion by birth (lineage) is permanent and lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Lineage/birth-purity is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Author cites Classical Athenian social/legal norms from centuries prior.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports social taunts and a father's legal status as a shared fact of the biography.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lack of full status is not the definition of being born in Athens.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Professional training is a resource for competence; lack thereof affects role quality.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific professional requirement (military).
scores.persistence_reasoning: A term of office as general is a significant long-term structural role.
scores.immutability_reasoning: General is a role-bound/episodic status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Anecdote from 5th/4th century BC Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a joke/witticism used to make a rhetorical point.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A general is not definitionally someone who lacks training.
